entries.summary: Milestone 10 (XGBoost model adapter + feature-importance explainer) implemented, plus an end-to-end run tab in the Streamlit UI that produces a business justification via the selected explanation provider.
work_done_till_date: Added ingestion/adapters/xgboost_adapter.py: XGBoostAdapter matches xgboost sklearn-API estimators and native Boosters purely by module path (no import-time xgboost dependency) and normalizes framework, task type, feature names and booster_details (n_estimators via num_boosted_rounds).
Registered XGBoostAdapter in ingestion/adapters/registry.py DEFAULT_ADAPTERS (after the sklearn tree adapter, before the generic fallback) and exported it from ingestion/adapters/__init__.py.
Made ModelAdapter._infer_task_type defensive so a native xgboost Booster (which breaks sklearn 1.6 is_classifier tag inspection) degrades gracefully instead of raising.

Added tools/explainers/xgboost_feature_importance.py: XGBoostFeatureImportanceExtractor exposes the same get_feature_importance/get_top_features interface using gain-based booster scores for both the sklearn API and native Boosters.

Extended tools/explainers/explainer_selector.py with is_xgboost() so select_feature_importance_explainer returns the XGBoost explainer for xgboost models and keeps the sklearn extractor for trees/forests.

Updated ui/app.py with an 'End-to-end run' tab: a provider selector (offline echo or openai) drives a full agent run via a temporary config override and renders the business justification, prediction, decision path and feature importance.

Added tests/ingestion/test_xgboost_adapter.py and tests/tools/test_xgboost_explainer.py (guarded with pytest.importorskip('xgboost')); installed xgboost in the venv and added it to requirements.txt. 75 tests pass across tools + agents + ingestion + prompts.

strengths: The platform now normalizes and explains XGBoost gradient-boosted models, selected automatically through the existing registry and explainer selector with no call-site changes.
XGBoost feature importance is gain-based and works for both the sklearn API and native Boosters via one shared interface.
The adapter recognizes xgboost by module path, so the registry imports and runs cleanly even when xgboost is not installed and the tests skip.
The UI now demonstrates the whole pipeline end to end and surfaces a plain-language business justification, switchable between offline echo and a live LLM.
gaps: XGBoost decision-path (per-tree) explanation is not implemented; only feature importance is covered for boosted models.
CatBoost, LightGBM, forecasting and optimization models are still unsupported.
Only echo and OpenAI explanation providers exist.
Pre-existing collection errors remain in scripts/test_tree_structure_exporter.py and test_open_ai.py (unrelated).

planned_next_step.title: Milestone 11: SHAP-based local explanations for tree and boosted models
planned_next_step.description: Add a model-agnostic local explanation capability using SHAP so a single prediction can be attributed to per-feature contributions for sklearn trees/forests and XGBoost, selected through the existing explainer selector and surfaced in the business-context prompt and UI.
deliverables: A SHAP-based local explainer under tools/explainers/ that computes per-feature contributions for a sample and exposes a stable interface (e.g. explain_instance()).
Extend the explainer selector to resolve the SHAP explainer for supported tree/boosted models, degrading gracefully when shap is not installed.
Incorporate the local contributions into the explanation prompt so the business justification cites the top drivers for the specific record.
tests for the SHAP explainer and selector, guarded to skip when shap is not installed.
definition_of_done: A fitted tree/forest/XGBoost model produces per-feature SHAP contributions for a single sample.
The selector returns the SHAP explainer for supported models and falls back cleanly when shap is absent.
The business justification references the record-specific top contributing features.
All new tests pass and the existing suites stay green.

entries.summary: Milestone 9 (end-to-end agent run + CLI entry point) implemented: a config-driven runner and main.py CLI execute the full compiled LangGraph agent on a question and sample, with passing end-to-end tests.
work_done_till_date: Added agents/agent_runner.py: build_initial_state assembles the ExplainabilityState from config path + question + optional sample (converted to a float numpy array), and run_agent invokes the compiled graph and returns the final state.
Rewrote main.py into an argparse CLI (--config, --question, --sample) that runs the agent and prints the explanation, or the branch output for single-intent runs.

Added tests/agents/test_agent_runner.py (4 tests) covering state assembly and end-to-end runs through the full pipeline (echo provider) and the feature_importance branch. 68 tests pass across the whole tests tree.

Verified the CLI end to end on configs/demo_config.yaml for both a single-intent question and the full pipeline; the enriched business-context prompt renders with feature meanings and project context.

strengths: The platform is now usable as one command (main.py) in addition to the UI and notebook.
The runner builds the initial state entirely from config plus inputs, with no hardcoded paths.
End-to-end runs are testable without network access via the echo provider, exercising real graph routing.
The full pipeline demonstrates the whole stack: ingestion, adapter metadata, routing, tree explainers, business-context prompt and provider.
gaps: Only the sklearn tree/forest adapter and tree explainers exist; XGBoost, CatBoost, forecasting and optimization models are still unsupported.
Only echo and OpenAI explanation providers exist.
The CLI takes a single sample inline; batch input and CSV/JSON sample loading are not supported.
Pre-existing collection errors remain in scripts/test_tree_structure_exporter.py and test_open_ai.py (unrelated).

planned_next_step.title: Milestone 10: XGBoost model adapter + feature-importance explainer
planned_next_step.description: Extend the platform beyond scikit-learn by adding an XGBoost adapter that normalizes gradient-boosted models and an explainer path for their feature importance, selected automatically through the existing adapter registry and explainer selector.
deliverables: An XGBoostAdapter under ingestion/adapters/ that matches xgboost sklearn-API models and native Boosters and normalizes metadata (framework, task type, n_estimators, feature names).
Register the adapter in the default registry ahead of the generic fallback.
Extend the explainer selector so XGBoost models resolve to a feature-importance explainer (gain/weight based).
tests for adapter matching/metadata and explainer selection, guarded to skip when xgboost is not installed.
definition_of_done: A fitted XGBoost classifier resolves to the XGBoost adapter with normalized metadata.
The explainer selector returns a working feature-importance explainer for XGBoost.
Unsupported/absent xgboost degrades gracefully (generic adapter) and tests skip cleanly when the package is missing.
All new tests pass and the existing suites stay green.

entries.summary: Milestone 8 (business-context-aware explanation prompts) implemented: the explanation prompt now weaves in data dictionary feature meanings and project context from the knowledge object, with passing unit tests.
work_done_till_date: Added prompts/explanation_prompt.py: build_explanation_prompt composes question, prediction, decision path and feature importance with a feature glossary (data dictionary meanings/units) and a Business Context block from the project context sections.
The builder gathers referenced features from both a single-tree decision path (path rules) and a forest path (feature_usage), plus feature importance, and renders meanings only for those features.
Missing knowledge sections are omitted gracefully: no feature glossary when the dictionary is absent, no Business Context block when context is empty.
Refactored agents/nodes.py so explanation_node builds the enriched prompt via prompts.explanation_prompt and passes it to the config-selected provider; removed the local prompt stub.

Added tests/prompts/ package with test_explanation_prompt.py (4 tests) covering feature-meaning inclusion, project-context inclusion, graceful omission, and forest feature_usage. 60 tests pass across tools + agents + ingestion + prompts.

strengths: Explanations are now grounded in the project's own semantic layer (feature meanings and business context), matching the enterprise 'translate to business language' goal.
The prompt builder is a pure function with no LLM/model calls, keeping the tools/prompts separation clean and fully unit-testable.
Handles single-tree and forest explanation shapes uniformly and degrades gracefully when the dictionary or context is missing.
The echo provider still returns the enriched prompt, so the full pipeline remains testable without a key.
gaps: Only echo and OpenAI explanation providers exist; other backends (Gemini, Anthropic, local) are not implemented.
Only the sklearn tree/forest adapter exists; XGBoost, CatBoost, forecasting and optimization adapters/explainers are still missing.
The prompt lists context sections verbatim without selecting the most relevant ones for the question.
Pre-existing collection errors remain in scripts/test_tree_structure_exporter.py and test_open_ai.py (unrelated).

planned_next_step.title: Milestone 9: End-to-end agent run + CLI entry point
planned_next_step.description: Provide a single, config-driven entry point that runs the full compiled LangGraph agent end to end (ingestion -> routing -> explanation) on a given question and sample, so the platform is usable as one command in addition to the UI and notebook.
deliverables: A runnable entry point (main.py and/or a scripts/run_agent.py) that loads config, invokes the compiled graph with a question and sample, and prints the explanation.
A thin agent-runner helper that assembles the initial ExplainabilityState from config + inputs.
Use the echo provider path so the run is testable without network access.
tests for the runner assembling state and executing a single-intent branch end to end on the demo model
definition_of_done: One command runs the whole graph on the demo config and prints an explanation.
The runner builds the initial state from config and CLI/inputs with no hardcoded paths.
An end-to-end test drives at least one intent branch through the compiled graph using the echo provider.
All new tests pass and the existing suites stay green.

entries.summary: Milestone 7 (config-driven, testable explanation provider) implemented: explanation_node now generates text via a provider selected from configuration, unit-tested with a fake provider and with no LLM calls at import time.
work_done_till_date: Added agents/explanation_provider.py: an ExplanationProvider interface, an EchoExplanationProvider (deterministic, no network), an OpenAIExplanationProvider (client imported/constructed lazily inside generate), and a build_provider(config) factory that reads explanation.provider and explanation.model.

Refactored agents/nodes.py: explanation_node renders the prompt via _build_explanation_prompt and delegates generation to the config-selected provider; removed the module-level openai import so no LLM work happens at import time.
Added an explanation section to configs/project_config.yaml (provider: openai) and to the demo config generator (provider: echo) so the demo/UI/notebook run without an API key.

Added tests/agents/test_explanation_provider.py (6 tests) covering the factory (echo/openai/unknown fallback, model from config) and explanation_node using the echo provider. 56 tests pass across tools + agents + ingestion; the graph still compiles.

strengths: explanation_node is now unit-tested end to end without network access via the echo provider.
The LLM model and provider are config-driven, matching the configuration-driven architecture rule.
No module performs LLM calls or client construction at import time, so all orchestration imports cleanly in tests.
Adding a new provider (e.g. Gemini/Anthropic) only requires a class plus a PROVIDERS entry.
gaps: Only echo and OpenAI providers exist; other backends (Gemini, Anthropic, local) are not implemented.
Only the sklearn tree/forest adapter exists; XGBoost, CatBoost, forecasting and optimization adapters/explainers are still missing.
The explanation prompt does not yet incorporate the data dictionary meanings or project context from the knowledge object.
Pre-existing collection errors remain in scripts/test_tree_structure_exporter.py and test_open_ai.py (unrelated).

planned_next_step.title: Milestone 8: Business-context-aware explanation prompts
planned_next_step.description: Enrich the explanation prompt with the semantic layer already in the knowledge object - data dictionary feature meanings/units and project context sections - so explanations translate technical outputs into business language grounded in the project's own documentation.
deliverables: A prompt-builder helper (under prompts/ or agents/) that composes question, prediction, decision path and feature importance with feature meanings from the data dictionary and relevant project-context sections.
Refactor explanation_node to use the enriched prompt builder fed from the knowledge object.
Map technical feature names to business descriptions in the decision path / feature importance rendering.
tests for the prompt builder covering presence of feature meanings and context, and graceful behaviour when the dictionary/context are missing
definition_of_done: The rendered prompt includes data dictionary meanings for the features referenced in the explanation.
Relevant project-context sections are included when available and omitted gracefully when not.
explanation_node produces the enriched prompt via the provider with no behavioural regression for the echo provider.
All new tests pass and the existing suites stay green.

entries.summary: Milestone 6 (forest explainability + adapter-aware tool selection) implemented: explanation tools now cover tree ensembles and are chosen from the normalized knowledge object, with passing unit tests.
work_done_till_date: Added tools/tree_reader/forest_path_extractor.py: ForestPathExtractor walks every estimator's decision path for a sample and aggregates per-feature usage across the ensemble, exposing the same extract_path() entry point as DecisionPathExtractor.

Added tools/explainers/explainer_selector.py: is_ensemble() plus select_decision_path_explainer() and select_feature_importance_explainer() choose the tree vs forest explainer from the knowledge object's tree_details (falling back to attribute inspection).
Refactored agents/nodes.py so decision_path_node and feature_importance_node use the selectors with the model metadata from the knowledge object instead of assuming a single DecisionTree.

Added tests/tools/test_explainer_selector.py (6 tests) covering metadata- and attribute-based ensemble detection, tree vs forest selection, forest path aggregation, and feature-importance for both. 50 tests pass across tools + agents + ingestion; the graph still compiles.
Confirmed via the demo/test suites that single-tree and forest paths both produce explanations without call-site changes.

strengths: Feature importance and a path-style explanation now work for both single trees and random forests.
The explainer is selected from the normalized knowledge object, keeping the agent nodes free of hardcoded model assumptions.
ForestPathExtractor and DecisionPathExtractor share one extract_path() interface, so the selector is a clean drop-in.
Adding a new explainer only requires extending the selector, not editing the nodes.
gaps: explanation_node still calls OpenAI directly, is hardcoded to a model name, and is not covered by tests.
Only the sklearn tree/forest adapter exists; XGBoost, CatBoost, forecasting and optimization adapters/explainers are still missing.
Forest path explanation returns per-estimator detail that could be large for big ensembles (no summarization cap yet).
Pre-existing collection errors remain in scripts/test_tree_structure_exporter.py and test_open_ai.py (unrelated).

planned_next_step.title: Milestone 7: Config-driven, testable explanation provider
planned_next_step.description: Decouple explanation_node from a hardcoded OpenAI client by introducing a small explanation-provider abstraction selected via configuration, so the LLM model is config-driven and the node can be unit-tested with a fake provider.
deliverables: An explanation provider interface plus an OpenAI-backed implementation and a fake/echo provider for tests, under tools/ or agents/.
A factory that builds the provider from configuration (provider name + model), with no import-time API calls.
Refactor explanation_node to render a prompt from the knowledge object and delegate generation to the injected provider.
tests for the provider factory and explanation_node using the fake provider
definition_of_done: explanation_node produces text via a provider chosen from config, with the model name read from config.
The node and provider factory are unit-tested without network access using a fake provider.
No module performs LLM calls at import time.
All new tests pass and the existing suites stay green.

entries.summary: Milestone 5 (intent-based router) implemented: a config-driven intent classifier and conditional routing dispatch a question to the relevant explanation branch, plus a Streamlit UI and a notebook to explore all work done to date.
work_done_till_date: Added tools/routing/intent_classifier.py: a pure, config-driven IntentClassifier that maps a question to a normalized intent via case-insensitive keyword matching (no LLM), with a default fallback.

Added agents/router.py: router_node (classifies via ConfigLoader + IntentClassifier and records state['intent']) and routing functions/maps (route_entry and post-node routers) driving conditional edges.

Rewired agents/explainability_graph.py: ingestion -> planner -> router, with conditional edges so a single-intent question runs only its branch and an unknown/general intent runs the full prediction -> decision_path -> feature_importance -> explanation pipeline.
Added intent to ExplainabilityState and a routing section (default_intent + keyword map) to configs/project_config.yaml.
Added scripts/generate_demo_artifacts.py which trains a fitted Iris decision tree and writes a data dictionary, project context and configs/demo_config.yaml.

Added notebooks/05_explainability_playground.ipynb and ui/app.py (Streamlit) to interactively exercise ingestion, adapters, validation, intent routing, decision path and feature importance; added streamlit to requirements.txt and installed it in the venv.

Added tests/tools/test_intent_classifier.py (6 tests) and tests/agents/test_router.py (5 tests). 44 tests pass across tools + agents + ingestion; end-to-end playground flow verified on the demo artifacts.

strengths: Questions are now routed to the relevant branch instead of always running the full linear pipeline; routing is entirely config-driven.
Unknown or general questions fall back safely to the full explanation pipeline.
A fitted demo model plus UI and notebook make every component built so far explorable without writing code.
The intent classifier is a pure tool (no LLM), keeping the tools/agents separation intact.
gaps: explanation_node still calls OpenAI directly and is not covered by tests.
Only the sklearn tree/forest adapter exists; XGBoost, CatBoost, forecasting and optimization adapters are still missing.
Decision-path and feature-importance tools are decision-tree specific; forests and other models are not yet explained.
Pre-existing collection errors remain in scripts/test_tree_structure_exporter.py and test_open_ai.py (unrelated).

planned_next_step.title: Milestone 6: Random forest explainability tools + adapter-aware tool selection
planned_next_step.description: Extend the explanation tools beyond single decision trees to tree ensembles (random forests / extra-trees) and select the right explainer based on the adapter/tree_details already produced during ingestion, so the router branches work for forests as well as single trees.
deliverables: A forest-aware decision path / feature importance capability (aggregate across estimators) under tools/.
An explainer-selection helper that picks the tree vs forest explainer from the knowledge object's adapter/tree_details.
Refactor the decision_path and feature_importance nodes to use the selector instead of assuming a single tree.
tests covering single-tree and forest explanation paths
definition_of_done: Feature importance and a path-style explanation work for both a single tree and a random forest.
The explainer is chosen from the normalized knowledge object, not hardcoded to DecisionTree.
The agent nodes produce explanations for a forest without code changes at the call site.
All new tests pass and the existing suites stay green.

entries.summary: Milestone 4 (wire ProjectKnowledge into the LangGraph pipeline) implemented: a config-driven ingestion node builds ProjectKnowledge as the graph entry state and downstream nodes consume the normalized knowledge object, with passing unit tests.
work_done_till_date: Rewrote agents/state.py into a clean ExplainabilityState TypedDict carrying config_path, knowledge, model and the existing explanation keys (fixed the previously invalid bare 'sample' line).
Added agents/ingestion_node.py: a config-driven entry node that reads ingestion.* paths via ConfigLoader, builds ProjectKnowledge, and populates state with the knowledge object and the live model.
Enhanced ProjectKnowledge to retain the live loaded model (self.model) so the pipeline reuses it without re-loading the artifact.

Refactored agents/nodes.py so prediction, decision_path and feature_importance nodes take only state and read the model and feature names from the knowledge object (with a data-dictionary fallback); made the OpenAI client lazy so the module imports without an API key.
Wired the ingestion node as the graph entry point in agents/explainability_graph.py (ingestion -> planner -> prediction -> decision_path -> feature_importance -> explanation).
Added an ingestion section to configs/project_config.yaml (model_path, data_dictionary_path, project_context_path; nulls allowed).
Added tests/agents/test_ingestion_node.py (3 tests); verified the compiled graph exposes the ingestion entry node. 33 tests pass across agents + ingestion suites.

strengths: The agent graph now starts from a ProjectKnowledge-populated state built entirely from config-driven paths.
Downstream nodes consume the normalized knowledge object and no longer receive model/feature args via broken partial bindings; the graph compiles end to end.
Missing or invalid artifacts surface through the validation report without crashing ingestion.
OpenAI usage is lazy, so orchestration modules import cleanly in tests without a key.
gaps: No intent-based router; every question runs the full linear pipeline regardless of what was asked.
Only the sklearn tree/forest adapter exists; XGBoost, CatBoost, forecasting and optimization adapters are still missing.
explanation_node still calls OpenAI directly and is not covered by tests.
Pre-existing collection errors remain in scripts/test_tree_structure_exporter.py and test_open_ai.py (unrelated).

planned_next_step.title: Milestone 5: Intent-based router in agent orchestration
planned_next_step.description: Introduce an intent classifier and conditional routing so a user question is dispatched to the relevant explanation branch (prediction, decision path, feature importance, or full explanation) instead of always running the entire linear pipeline.
deliverables: A tool that classifies a question into a normalized intent (config-driven intent set, no LLM in the tool).
A router node plus conditional edges in agents/explainability_graph.py that select the branch from the classified intent.
Update ExplainabilityState to carry the resolved intent.
tests for the intent classifier and the routing decisions
definition_of_done: A question resolves to a normalized intent and the graph routes to the matching branch.
An unknown or general intent falls back to the full explanation pipeline.
Routing is driven by configuration, not hardcoded question strings.
All new tests pass and the existing suites stay green.

entries.summary: Milestone 3 (model framework adapter architecture) implemented: a plugin adapter layer with a base interface, registry, sklearn tree/forest adapter and generic fallback, wired into ModelArtifactLoader, with passing unit tests.
work_done_till_date: Added ingestion/adapters/ package: base ModelAdapter interface, GenericModelAdapter fallback, SklearnTreeAdapter, and AdapterRegistry with a default_registry() factory.
Refactored the model metadata normalization helpers (task type, feature names, classes, feature count, hyperparameters) out of ModelArtifactLoader into the base ModelAdapter.
SklearnTreeAdapter matches single trees (tree_) and forest ensembles (RandomForest/ExtraTrees) and adds framework and tree_details (depth, n_leaves, node_count, or n_estimators).
AdapterRegistry.resolve() selects the first matching specialized adapter and falls back to GenericModelAdapter; register() gives user adapters priority.
Wired the registry into ModelArtifactLoader.extract_metadata(), which now delegates to the resolved adapter; loader accepts an optional registry.
Added tests/ingestion/test_model_adapters.py (8 tests) covering adapter resolution, tree/forest details, generic fallback, custom-adapter priority and loader integration.
Full ingestion suite green: 30 tests pass.

strengths: Model normalization is now pluggable per framework without touching the loader.
Unsupported models degrade gracefully via the generic adapter instead of raising.
Existing metadata contract preserved (added 'adapter' plus tree-specific fields), so downstream consumers keep working.
gaps: Only the sklearn tree/forest adapter is implemented; XGBoost, CatBoost, forecasting and optimization adapters are not yet written.
No intent-based router in agent orchestration.
The ProjectKnowledge object is not yet consumed by the LangGraph agent pipeline.
Pre-existing collection errors remain in scripts/test_tree_structure_exporter.py and test_open_ai.py (unrelated to ingestion).

planned_next_step.title: Milestone 4: Wire ProjectKnowledge into the LangGraph agent pipeline
planned_next_step.description: Consume the unified ProjectKnowledge object (model metadata via adapters, feature records, context sections, validation report) as the entry state of the LangGraph explainability agent, replacing any ad-hoc model loading in the orchestration layer.
deliverables: Update agents/state.py to carry the ProjectKnowledge object (or its normalized sections).
An ingestion node that builds ProjectKnowledge from configured artifact paths and populates agent state.
Refactor existing nodes to read model/feature/context data from the knowledge object instead of loading models directly.
tests for the ingestion node and the updated agent state
definition_of_done: The agent graph starts from a ProjectKnowledge-populated state built from config-driven paths.
Downstream nodes consume the normalized knowledge object rather than re-loading artifacts.
Missing or invalid artifacts surface through the validation report without crashing the graph.
All new tests pass and the existing ingestion suite stays green.

entries.summary: Milestone 2 (unified project knowledge object) implemented: ProjectKnowledge aggregator composes the three ingestion loaders and the validator behind a single entry point, with passing unit tests.
work_done_till_date: Added ingestion/project_knowledge.py with the ProjectKnowledge aggregator that runs ModelArtifactLoader, DataDictionaryLoader and ProjectContextLoader from a single build() entry point.
build() returns one normalized knowledge object with keys model, features, context and validation (the ArtifactValidator report).
Loader paths are optional; missing artifacts are skipped gracefully (model=None, features=[], context={}) and surfaced through the validation report instead of raising.
Exposed ProjectKnowledge via ingestion/__init__.py public API.
Added tests/ingestion/test_project_knowledge.py (4 tests) covering full composition, no-artifact graceful path, model-only path and instance-attribute population.
Full ingestion suite green: 22 tests pass.

strengths: Downstream agents and tools now obtain all three normalized artifacts plus the validation report from one call.
Partial knowledge (any subset of artifacts) is supported without raising, enabling graceful agent handling.
Aggregator reuses the existing loaders and validator with no duplication of normalization logic.
gaps: No plugin adapter architecture yet for multiple ML frameworks (XGBoost, CatBoost, forecasting, optimization).
No intent-based router in agent orchestration.
Knowledge object is not yet consumed by the LangGraph agent pipeline.
Pre-existing collection errors remain in scripts/test_tree_structure_exporter.py and test_open_ai.py (unrelated to ingestion).

planned_next_step.title: Milestone 3: Model framework adapter (plugin) architecture
planned_next_step.description: Introduce a plugin adapter layer so the platform can recognize and normalize models from multiple frameworks (sklearn trees/forests, XGBoost, CatBoost, forecasting, optimization) behind a single adapter interface, selected automatically from the loaded model.
deliverables: ingestion/adapters/ package with a base ModelAdapter interface and a registry
A default sklearn tree/forest adapter refactored from the current metadata extraction
Adapter selection wired into ModelArtifactLoader / ProjectKnowledge
tests for adapter selection and per-adapter metadata extraction
definition_of_done: A base adapter interface plus a registry that resolves the correct adapter from a loaded model.
At least the sklearn tree/forest adapter implemented and selected automatically.
Unsupported models fall back to generic metadata extraction without raising.
All new adapter tests pass and the existing ingestion suite stays green.

entries.summary: Milestone 1 (ingestion layer) implemented: model, data dictionary and project context loaders plus artifact validator, all with passing unit tests.
work_done_till_date: Created the ingestion package with a unified public API (ModelArtifactLoader, DataDictionaryLoader, ProjectContextLoader, ArtifactValidator).
ModelArtifactLoader loads .pkl and .joblib artifacts, unwraps sklearn Pipelines, and extracts normalized metadata (model type, task type, hyperparameters, feature names, classes).
DataDictionaryLoader parses .xlsx (openpyxl) and .csv into normalized feature records via alias-based column canonicalization.
ProjectContextLoader reads .md and .txt, exposes an extensible reader registry for .pdf/.docx, and splits markdown into sections.
ArtifactValidator produces a structured report (valid flag, errors, warnings, summary) and cross-checks model features against the data dictionary.
Added tests/ingestion suite (18 tests) covering all four modules; added openpyxl to requirements.txt.

strengths: Dedicated ingestion layer now normalizes the three required input artifacts into consistent outputs.
Validator surfaces gaps and inconsistencies without raising, enabling graceful agent handling.
All new ingestion tests pass in isolation.
gaps: No unified project knowledge object yet composing the three loader outputs.
No plugin adapter architecture for multiple ML frameworks.
No intent-based router in agent orchestration.
Pre-existing collection errors remain in scripts/test_tree_structure_exporter.py and test_open_ai.py (unrelated to ingestion).

planned_next_step.title: Milestone 2: Build unified project knowledge object
planned_next_step.description: Compose the normalized ingestion outputs (model metadata, feature records, context sections) into a single project knowledge object consumed by downstream agents and tools.
deliverables: ingestion/project_knowledge.py (aggregator over the three loaders + validator)
tests for the project knowledge aggregator
definition_of_done: Aggregator runs all three loaders and the validator from a single entry point.
Returns one normalized knowledge object plus the validation report.
Gracefully handles any missing artifact.
All new tests pass.

entries.summary: Architecture review completed and redesign direction agreed toward an enterprise, reusable explainability platform.
work_done_till_date: Established Phase 1 tooling for decision tree explainability (model loading, tree reading, decision path extraction, feature importance extraction, tree export, tree visualization).
Implemented initial LangGraph orchestration with planner -> prediction -> decision_path -> feature_importance -> explanation pipeline.
Added foundational configuration and unit tests for core tools.
Defined architecture constraints in AGENTS.md (configuration-driven, no hardcoded assumptions, tools-first, tests-first).
Captured enterprise redesign requirements in context.md: ingest 3 user artifacts (model artifact, data dictionary, project context) and infer everything else.

strengths: Tool-first modular foundation is in place.
Initial orchestration and test scaffold exist.
Clear product vision and architecture rules are documented.
gaps: No dedicated ingestion layer for the 3 required input artifacts.
No unified project knowledge object built from model/data dictionary/context.
No plugin adapter architecture yet for multiple ML frameworks.
No intent-based router in agent orchestration yet.

planned_next_step.title: Milestone 1: Build ingestion layer
planned_next_step.description: Implement ingestion modules to load, normalize, and validate model artifact, data dictionary, and project context into one normalized ingestion output.
deliverables: ingestion/model_loader.py
ingestion/data_dictionary_loader.py
ingestion/project_context_loader.py
ingestion/artifact_validator.py
tests for each ingestion module
definition_of_done: Supports .pkl and .joblib model files.
Parses data dictionary from .xlsx (and .csv fallback) into normalized feature records.
Reads project context from .md and .txt with extensible reader interface for pdf/docx.
Validator returns structured validation report with errors/warnings.
All new ingestion tests pass.
